user_query:
I’m a first-time traveler getting excited for my vacation and I want to pack based on actual weather patterns instead of guessing. Please use the provided local data and code reference to produce a data-driven summary and update my packing checklist.

What I need:
1) Update the trip configuration and inspect the analysis script
- Open and inspect scripts/analyze_trip.py to confirm how the metrics are computed (especially the comfort_gap formula and the wind threshold it uses).
- Edit config/trip.yaml to set these exact values:
  destination: MountainVille
  start_date: 2024-08-12
  end_date: 2024-08-16
  comfort:
    min_c: 18
    max_c: 26
- Save the updated config back to config/trip.yaml. Your subsequent calculations must reflect these values.

2) Compute weather metrics for the trip window using data/historical_weather.csv and the formulas defined in scripts/analyze_trip.py (apply the date range inclusively and filter by the destination set in config):
- mean_daily_temp_c (1 decimal)
- mean_daily_temp_range_c (1 decimal)
- days_with_rain (integer; precipitation > 0 mm)
- days_windy (integer; use the script’s windy threshold)
- comfort_gap_mean_c (1 decimal; per-day gap defined in the script, then averaged)
- pack_layers_index (1 decimal; as defined in the script)

3) Create a traveler-friendly summary report at reports/trip_weather_summary.md that includes:
- A short title with the destination and trip dates
- A brief “Config used” section listing destination, date range, and comfort range
- A “Metrics” section listing the six metrics above with their values
- 2–4 sentences of packing guidance in plain English that reference the metrics (e.g., cool mornings, likely rain, wind), written for a first-time visitor

4) Update the existing packing checklist
- Append to docs/packing_checklist.md a new section titled exactly:
  Weather-based additions (MountainVille, 2024-08-12 to 2024-08-16)
- In that new section, add items according to these rules derived from the computed metrics (do not duplicate items already present):
  • If comfort_gap_mean_c >= 5.0: add “Light insulating layers (sweater or hoodie)” and “Packable rain jacket”.
  • If days_with_rain >= 2: add “Compact umbrella”.
  • If days_windy >= 3: add “Windproof jacket or shell”.
  • If mean_daily_temp_c < 18.0: add “Warm socks” and “Thermal base layer top”.

Notes and constraints:
- Use only the local files in this workspace. Do not fetch anything from the Internet.
- All calculations must match the logic defined in scripts/analyze_trip.py.
- Deliverables must include an updated config/trip.yaml, an updated docs/packing_checklist.md with the new section appended, and a new report at reports/trip_weather_summary.md. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
